danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite characters positioned in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard; damage on contact unless approached from above

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprite with distinct animation patterns; health bar display (bossHP memory register 0xB9)
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger threshold; requires pattern learning and multiple hits to defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen transition gaps; vertical drops in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death or level restart; avoidable through rope mechanics or precise jumping

danger_signals.visual_form: Static terrain features requiring cane interaction or alternate movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Progression barriers; indicate areas requiring specific tools or upgrades from Gyro Gearloose

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprite items scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation; enables purchases at stores for recovery items and upgrades

safety_signals.visual_form: Character encounter point; NPC interaction zone
safety_signals.meaning: Cane upgrade availability; grants access to new areas and hidden treasures via tool enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive rope sprites that Scrooge can grab and traverse
safety_signals.meaning: Safe vertical traversal method; prevents fall damage and enables Y subpixel manipulation for sequence optimization
Non-mandatory collection goal; six pieces unlock optional sixth stage with lost McDuck treasure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cane weapon; McDuck family lineage (Fergus McDuck connection via torn map piece)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Levels designed around Scrooge's travel itinerary; areas represent global expedition (Niagara Falls, Bermuda Triangle, Mu, Egypt, Scotland)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: D-1000 shapeshifting robot (final boss form); archenemy status established in narrative
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot transformation; appears as kidnapper NPC before revealing as D-1000 mechanical form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pirate ship in Bermuda Triangle serves as final confrontation arena; treasure pursuit motivation creates competitive dynamic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Huey (quest initiator with torn map), Webby (hostage), Launchpad (treasure recovery)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Family-centric narrative framing; treasure belongs to Fergus McDuck (father); safe endings prioritize family over wealth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level selection screen displays available stages with unique graphical identities per world (Egypt pyramids, Scotland terrain, water-based Bermuda Triangle aesthetic)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can select levels in any order (non-linear progression); no level-gating except final optional sixth stage requiring all map pieces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level remains accessible with same visual appearance; treasure chest indicators persist if map pieces remain uncollected
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can revisit completed levels to collect additional money and items; cane upgrades from Gyro enable access to previously unreachable areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss enemy sprite despawns; arena becomes passable; bossHP register (0xB9) decrements with each hit; bossInv register (0xB8) tracks invulnerability frames during damage animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss can only take damage from above (collision glitch allows side damage with precise positioning); defeat triggers level completion state and stage exit availability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sixth optional stage becomes available in level select menu; represents hidden treasure location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocks final dungeon progression; alternative ending path where Scrooge reveals treasure hidden under hat, avoiding Glomgold's theft

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Money counter in HUD reflects depletion during shop purchases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Triggers bad ending if player reaches final boss with zero remaining funds; Glomgold finds treasure and becomes famous, infuriating Scrooge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final stage environment transforms to pirate ship battle arena; Glomgold/D-1000 reveals transformation visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forces confrontation with shapeshifter robot; creates time-pressure narrative (ransom condition); sinking ship sequence triggers escape segment

environmental_storytelling.example: Five explorable regions (Niagara Falls, Bermuda Triangle pirate ship, Mu, Egypt, Scotland) correspond to real-world and mythological locations, establishing Scrooge's globe-trotting adventurer identity established in animated series

Each level contains distinct treasure artifact appropriate to location (Egyptian artifacts in Egypt stage, underwater treasures in Bermuda Triangle); collected treasures become ransom payment in final confrontation, showing narrative consequence of exploration

Torn map piece from Huey initiates quest; five more pieces hidden in treasure chests throughout stages; collection requirement drives player toward optional sixth stage with McDuck family treasure, creating tangible progression reward tied to lore

Gyro Gearloose appears as upgrade vendor; enables cane modifications that unlock hidden areas; positions progress gating behind tool enhancement rather than level progression, matching game's exploration-focused design

Boss encounters guardian-role enemies (e.g., stage-specific creatures) that represent obstacles to treasure collection; defeated bosses' removal opens stage completion, visually confirming obstacle clearance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Horizontal speed ~1.x pixels per frame; X subpixel advances 4/256ths per frame with rolling behavior; wall collision causes subpixel rollover without stopping movement (enables positioning exploits)

Cane jump action easier to perform than original; Y subpixel advances only while on rope or falling through screen transitions; jump cancellation possible by pressing B for 4 frames after landing, enabling instant forward movement
Can hang from interactive hooks; Y subpixel manipulation occurs during rope traversal; enables vertical movement between platforms with safe damage avoidance

Vulnerable only to damage from above (standard collision rule); can be damaged from side if positioned precisely relative to enemy movement (requires enemy positioning to enable collision glitch); damage via cane jump attack
Triggers RNG roll if enemy can drop items; RNG advances via GlobalTimer (0x8F) addition to RNG register (0x90) with increment; frame-precise enemy kill timing manipulates item drops

bossHP (0xB9) tracks health points; bossInv (0xB8) tracks invulnerability frames post-damage; X position factors into RNG mixing for attack pattern generation; multiple hits required; defeat removes boss sprite and enables stage exit
Appears as kidnapper NPC initially; transforms into mechanical robot form during final confrontation; signals narrative twist where ransom is false objective, true threat is destruction protocol

Require running into object to initialize 'hockey hit'; alternative: move along same platform for 15 frames, insert blank frame at 16/32-frame intervals to enable immediate interaction upon reaching object
Facing backward for correct frame count triggers zip glitch; gains ~10 pixels forward traversal; demonstrates level geometry exploitability

notes: DuckTales 2 uses non-linear stage selection (Mega Man-style) with reversitable level design enabling resource grinding and cane upgrade integration. World-state communication relies on NPC interactions (Gyro shop unlocks), collectible status (map pieces), and narrative framing (family vs. treasure moral choice in endings). Danger signals are primarily enemy/boss presence with collision rules; safety is conveyed through rope mechanics, upgrade availability, and treasure/money pickup density. Faction identity is weak (no color-coded teams) but narrative-driven: McDuck family vs. Glomgold competition for Fergus McDuck's hidden treasure creates zero-sum ending condition where player choice (spending money vs. hoarding) determines good vs. bad ending. The torn map piece, Webby kidnapping, and pirate ship ransoming create political/dramatic stakes elevating simple platformer to quest narrative.
